title: aspalathus linearis subsp. pinifolia
intro: Aspalathus linearis subsp. pinifolia, also known as Rooibos Pine or Rooibos Needle Bush, is a subspecies of the Aspalathus linearis plant, native to the Western Cape province in South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the Rooibos tea, which contains antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that have been linked to several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Aspalathus linearis subsp. pinifolia are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and aspalathin, a unique xanthone that is believed to have anti-diabetic properties. Rooibos tea is often prepared by infusing the leaves in hot water or by combining them with other herbs, and can be consumed as a hot or cold beverage.
